subPoints: Good use of Pandas
Good use of the Pandas' functions:
Correlation analysis - well done
Plotting - good, concise coding for the plot generation
delimiter (i.e., `sep`) - incorrectly specified the separator used within the CSV-formatted file
header (i.e., `header`) - incorrectly specified the header line
input data - incorrectly combined the data - combining the data to give 6497 rows (e.g., via `concat` or `merge`)
input data - incorrectly read in the data
input file - renaming the input data file is not a good idea here
input file path - hard coding your local folder structure for the input file path results in problems, requiring the user to edit your notebook
new columns - incorrectly added the wine types to the dataframes
cleaning - the data was not cleaned properly (i.e., `drop`)
`drop_duplicates` - you did not save the results (e.g., using `inplace` or assigning it to a new object)
`dropna` - you did not save the results (e.g., using `inplace` or assigning it to a new object)
final number of rows after `drop_duplicates` and `dropna` should be 5286

negative.mainPoint: Displaying a dataframe as a table
subPoints: A dataframe in a Jupyter-notebook should be displayed by either using the dataframe's name by itself, or using `display`.
Printing a dataframe does not make use Jupyter-notebook's capability of displaying an easily readable table.
`corr` - did not use Pandas' built-in correlation function
correlation analysis - done incorrectly`
identification - the maximum negative correlation category was not identified`
identification - incorrectly identified the largest negative correlation category - should have been `density`
meaning of correlation - incorrectly stated
meaning of correlation - none given
meaning of correlation - poorly done
library usage - imported additional libraries for plotting (e.g., imported matplotlib)
no plots given
plot colors - poorly chosen
plot type - a histogram was not plotted
plot title(s) - none given
fontsize - could have been done more appropriately (e.g., a size of 14 to 18)
axis labels - missing labels
axis labels - not well labeled
axis labels - units not provided
data was not sorted
incorrect data sorting (e.g., by quality and alcohol)
not saved - the data sorting was not saved (e.g., assigning as a new object)
dataframes - creating multiple dataframes for this solution was not necessary - three would have been reasonable (e.g., red_wines, white_wines, combined_wines)
Not saved - the final data was not saved to a file
